text.title: Template Spec Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Template Spec. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Template carries semver; updates bump version per semver rules.
Section headers cannot be removed without major-version bump.
Placeholder copy demonstrates the expected depth; lorem ipsum rejected.
Template passes the spec-lint ruleset (section presence, FR numbering, no placeholders).
rule.rationale: Templates that fail lint pollute downstream specs.

rule.statement: Template reviewed quarterly; stale fields removed.
Major version bumps maintain back-compat for 30 days; old specs flagged but not blocked.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-template-spec.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
